docs.text: Prerequisites ¶ This page defines the baseline setup required before running the public release. Tested Environment ¶ The following environment has been tested: Ubuntu 22.04 OpenCV 4.5.4 C++17 compiler (GCC/Clang) CMake 3.16+ Newer Ubuntu and OpenCV versions may work, but are not verified. Note: If you have trouble with environment setup, we recommend using Docker . Required Dependencies ¶ Install core packages: sudo apt-get update sudo apt-get install -y build-essential cmake pkg-config libopencv-dev libhdf5-dev libeigen3-dev Install Ceres Solver (2.2.0 recommended) using the official guide: http://ceres-solver.org/installation.html Input Preconditions ¶ Input HDF5 must include the following dataset layout: / └── events ├── ts (uint64, microseconds) ├── xs (uint16) ├── ys (uint16) └── ps (uint8, 0 or 1) ts , xs , ys , and ps must be one-dimensional arrays of equal length. ts must be integer microseconds ( uint64 ).
docs.title: Prerequisites
